name: 3-Body Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: Conservation of total energy and momentum bounding the accessible phase space volume

VINIT.justification: The void before masses are placed or time begins, representing an uninitialized physical state

TANCH.justification: The closed boundary preserving phase space volume, containing the entire dynamical system

AFWD.justification: Directed transformation of the dynamical state forward in time via symplectic flow

AREV.justification: Reversing the momentum vectors to trace the orbit backward, testing contravariant descent

CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining of discrete time-step propagators to build continuous evolution

IMSCRIB.justification: Total energy and angular momentum, the state properties that remain identical to themselves throughout evolution

FSPLIT.justification: Splits the absolute 3-body state into Center of Mass motion and Relative internal motion

FFUSE.justification: Reconstitutes the absolute 3-body state exactly from the Center of Mass and Relative coordinates

EVALT.justification: Affirmative state where closed-form, stable, or periodic solutions exist (e.g., Figure-8, Lagrange)

EVALF.justification: Negative state where trajectories diverge exponentially and no closed-form solution exists

ENGAGR.justification: A paradice where stable invariant tori and chaotic seas coexist simultaneously in phase space without resolution

IFIX.justification: Permanent, irreversible record of trajectory intersections with a specific phase-space plane

phase_2.split_input: Absolute 3-Body State (positions and momenta of 3 masses)
phase_2.fuse_result: Absolute 3-Body State (exact recovery of original positions and momenta)

phase_3.void_description: Pre-Big Bang or empty space with no masses, gravitational fields, or initial conditions defined
phase_3.true_description: Stable, integrable, or periodic orbital configurations such as Euler collinear, Lagrange equilateral, or Figure-8 orbits
phase_3.false_description: Chaotic, non-integrable trajectories exhibiting sensitive dependence on initial conditions and eventual ejection of a body
phase_3.both_description: Mixed phase space regions where KAM invariant tori (stable) and chaotic separatrices (unstable) coexist indefinitely

steps.domain_action: Initialize the empty phase space with no masses or initial conditions, establishing the physical void
Define the three point masses and their intrinsic properties, establishing their self-identity
Assign initial position and momentum vectors to the three bodies, moving from void to a defined initial state
Apply Jacobi coordinate transformation, splitting the absolute state into T-arm (Center of Mass) and F-arm (Relative coordinates)
Anchor the T-arm; the Center of Mass moves uniformly, representing a trivially integrable success state
Propagate the Center of Mass forward in time via uniform linear motion along the T-arm
Anchor the F-arm; the Relative coordinates contain the complex, non-linear gravitational interactions representing non-integrability
Inside the F-arm, split the relative Hamiltonian into inner T-arm (integrable Keplerian 2-body) and inner F-arm (non-integrable perturbation)
Anchor the inner T-arm; the Keplerian part admits closed-form elliptical solutions
Propagate the unperturbed Keplerian orbits forward in time along the inner T-arm
Anchor the inner F-arm; the perturbation part represents the chaotic 3-body coupling
Apply time-reversal to the perturbation to test symmetry, revealing exponential divergence along the inner F-arm
Inner fuse; recombine the Keplerian and perturbation branches to reconstitute the full relative Hamiltonian
Enter the KAM regime within the relative state, holding both stable invariant tori and chaotic seas in simultaneous paradice
Chain the symplectic integration steps to evolve the full relative state over a finite time interval
Record the trajectory intersections on a Poincaré surface of section, permanently fixing the chaotic map
Outer fuse; apply the inverse Jacobi transformation, recombining the evolved Center of Mass (T-arm) and Relative state (F-arm) into the absolute 3-Body state
Verify the constants of motion (total energy, total angular momentum) remain identical to their initial values
Close the system with Liouville's Theorem, confirming the total phase space volume is strictly conserved and bounded

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Hamiltonian mechanics formalism translating physical forces into symplectic phase-space flows
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Newtonian gravitational coupling (inverse-square law) transmitting force between mass pairs
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Phase space coordinates (positions and conjugate momenta) storing the complete dynamical state
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Symplectic integrator (e.g., Leapfrog or Runge-Kutta-Nyström) determining discrete time-step evolution
phase_5.alfs_store: The N-body Hamiltonian and Newton's laws of motion, the canonical axioms governing the system

phase_6.cycle_cost: Computational precision and phase-space volume resolution consumed per integration step
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Initial phase space volume element at t=0
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Evolved phase space volume element at t=delta_t
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — Liouville's theorem guarantees the phase space volume is strictly conserved under Hamiltonian flow, making the cycle entropy-conservative

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → IMSCRIB → AFWD → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → EVALF → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → EVALF → AREV → FFUSE → ENGAGR → CLINK → IFIX → FFUSE → IMSCRIB → TANCH
-- Class: 3-Body Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(9,4,5,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=19
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(7, 12), (3, 16)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑠  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑼 → 𐑾  | identity — self-imscription
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑠 → 𐑚  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [4] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [5] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [6] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [7] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [8] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [9] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [10] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [11] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [12] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑙  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [13] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [14] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [15] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑚 → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [16] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑠  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [17] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑙 → 𐑡  | identity — self-imscription
--   [18] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑠 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def 3_body_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑠)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Initialize the empty phase space with no masses or initial conditions, establ...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Define the three point masses and their intrinsic properties, establishing th...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑠 𐑚)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Assign initial position and momentum vectors to the three bodies, moving from...)
  -- FSPLIT [3] (gran := 𐑚) (Apply Jacobi coordinate transformation, splitting the absolute state into T-arm (Center of Mass) and F-arm (Relative coordinates)) / FFUSE [16] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Anchor the T-arm; the Center of Mass moves uniformly, representing a triviall...)
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Propagate the Center of Mass forward in time via uniform linear motion along ...)
      -- F-branch (4 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Anchor the F-arm; the Relative coordinates contain the complex, non-linear gr...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [13] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Enter the KAM regime within the relative state, holding both stable invariant...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [14] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Chain the symplectic integration steps to evolve the full relative state over...)
        (.arrow 𐑭 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [15] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Record the trajectory intersections on a Poincaré surface of section, permane...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [16]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [16] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Outer fuse; apply the inverse Jacobi transformation, recombining the evolved Center of Mass (T-arm) and Relative state (F-arm) into the absolute 3-Body state)
  -- FSPLIT [7] (gran := 𐑚) (Inside the F-arm, split the relative Hamiltonian into inner T-arm (integrable Keplerian 2-body) and inner F-arm (non-integrable perturbation)) / FFUSE [12] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Anchor the inner T-arm; the Keplerian part admits closed-form elliptical solu...)
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Propagate the unperturbed Keplerian orbits forward in time along the inner T-arm)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [10] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Anchor the inner F-arm; the perturbation part represents the chaotic 3-body c...)
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [11] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Apply time-reversal to the perturbation to test symmetry, revealing exponenti...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [12]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑙)  -- [12] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Inner fuse; recombine the Keplerian and perturbation branches to reconstitute the full relative Hamiltonian)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑙 𐑡)  -- [17] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Verify the constants of motion (total energy, total angular momentum) remain ...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑠 𐑼)  -- [18] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Close the system with Liouville's Theorem, confirming the total phase space v...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def 3_body_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (3_body_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def 3_body_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (3_body_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem 3_body_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
